description: Equilibria Game Engine resources and functionality.

categories.Elements: Equilibria physical game elements, such as aliens, lifts, and pick-ups, terrain, and other objects that can be placed in a world.
categories.Graphics: Resources for presenting the game world to the player.
categories.Logic: Resources for controlling the behaviour of the game world.

CameraGameplay.description: A camera shows a single zone at a time, with ability to view the zone from different isometric angles.

direction.name: Initial Angle
direction.description: Angle at which this camera is initially set.

rotateDuration.name: Rotate Duration
rotateDuration.description: The time it takes to rotate the camera from one angle to another (in ms).

CameraLinked.description: A camera copies the state of another view's camera.

CameraOverview.description: A camera that shows an entire world from above.

CameraTransitional.description: A camera that transitions between two other configured cameras.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: The time it takes to transition between the two states (in ms).

onStartDeparture.name: On Departure from State A
onStartDeparture.description: Action to perform when this camera leaves state A.

onEndArrival.name: On Arrival at State B
onEndArrival.description: Action to perform when this camera reaches state B.

onEndDeparture.name: On Departure from State B
onEndDeparture.description: Action to perform when this camera leaves state B.

onStartArrival.name: On Arrival at State A
onStartArrival.description: Action to perform when this camera reaches state A.

CameraVariant.description: A camera that combines the states of other cameras.

location.name: Location
location.description: Use the location of the specified camera to position this camera.

yaw.name: Angle
yaw.description: Use the angle of the specified camera to rotate this camera.

pitch.name: Tilt
pitch.description: Use the tilt of the specified camera to tilt this camera.

zoom.name: Zoom
zoom.description: Use the zoom of the specified camera to zoom this camera.

SurfacePatternComposite.description: A composite pattern that combines multiple surface patterns.

SurfacePatternOutline.description: Renders outlines around surfacess to give them a cartoonish appearance.

SurfacePatternSplitVariant.description: Renders surfaces using different styles depending on whether the tiles are split into triangles.

regular.name: Regular Pattern
regular.description: Pattern to use for tiles without a split.

splitA.name: Split A Pattern
splitA.description: Pattern to use for one half of split tiles.

splitB.name: Split B Pattern
splitB.description: Pattern to use for other half of split tiles.

SurfacePatternTile.description: Renders surfaces using a single texture.

WallPatternCap.description: Renders walls with using different textures at the top, bottom, and middle sections.

top.name: Top
top.description: Texture applied to top part of walls.

middle.name: Middle
middle.description: Texture applied between top and bottom part of walls.

bottom.name: Bottom
bottom.description: Texture applied to bottom part of walls.

WallPatternComposite.description: A composite pattern that combines multiple wall patterns.

WallPatternOutline.description: Renders outlines around walls to give them a cartoonish appearance.

WallPatternTile.description: Renders walls using a single texture.

ZoneViewTypeOverview.description: Renders coloured squares to represent zones.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitBoundary.description: Applies a boundary to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitCellLocation.description: Applies a cell location to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitChaser.description: Applies a chase behaviour to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitModel.description: Applies a visual representation to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitMovable.description: Applies the ability to move to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitSpinner.description: Applies a visual representation to this entity that spins as it moves.

Alien.description: Physical entities that chases a specific target, capable of triggering collisions with other entities.

appearance.name: Appearance
appearance.description: Model to visually represent this type of alien.

target.name: Target
target.description: Target that aliens of this type will attempt to move towards

acceleration.name: Acceleration
acceleration.description: Rate at which the aliens of this type increase their movement speed

friction.name: Friction
friction.description: Rate at which acceleration drops off for aliens of this type.

spinSpeed.name: Spin Speed
spinSpeed.description: Speed at which aliens of this type spin as they move.  This is purely a visual effect.

height.name: Height
height.description: Height of aliens of this type in number of platform blocks.

radius.name: Radius
radius.description: Radius of aliens of this type in number of platform blocks.

hugMomentum.name: Hug Momentum Threshold
hugMomentum.description: Speed under which aliens of this type will stick to a wall and slide along it instead of bouncing off it.

BoundaryHandler.description: Define actions to be performed when objects of a specific type enter or leave fixed boundaries of a specific type.  This can be used to define on players encountering different types of pick-ups, or upon entering and leaving zones.

object.name: Entity Type
object.description: Type of entity to react upon boundary entry and exit.

boundary.name: Boundary Type
boundary.description: Type of boundary to test entry and exit of.

onEntry.name: Action on Entry
onEntry.description: Action to perform each time an entity of the selected type enters a boundary of the selected type.

onExit.name: Action on Exit
onExit.description: Action to perform each time an entity of the selected type leaving a boundary of the selected type.

CollisionHandler.description: Define actions to be performed when objects of one type collide with object of another type.  This can be used to apply damage to the player upon collision with alien crafts.

objectA.name: Entity Type A
objectA.description: React upon collisions between entities of this type and Entity Type B.

objectB.name: Entity Type B
objectB.description: React upon collisions between entities of this type and Entity Type A.

onCollision.name: Action on Collision
onCollision.description: Action to perform each time two entities of each respective type begin to overlap

onParting.name: Action on Separation
onParting.description: Action to perform each time two entities of each respective type cease to overlap.

Lift.description: Vertically moving platforms that enable players and other objects to reach higher places.

appearance.name: Appearance
appearance.description: Model to visually represent lifts of this type.

state.name: State
state.description: Determine whether all lifts of this type are moving or not.

onTick.name: Action on Movement
onTick.description: Action to perform upon movement of the lift.  The action is triggered each time the lift begins movement from or passes through a whole block height.

ModelCycler.description: Models that programmatically cycle through the appearances of other models.

model.name: Model
model.description: A model to cycle through within this set.

models.name: Model
models.description: Add another model to cycle through within this set.

PickUp.description: Fixed-place objects that can be collected by players or other objects.  Note that interaction between entity type and pick-up types can be defined in Boundary Handlers.

appearance.name: Appearance
appearance.description: Model to visually represent pick-ups of this type.

Player.description: Crafts whose movements are controlled through external inputs.

radius.name: Radius
radius.description: Radius of players of this type in number of platform blocks.

height.name: Height
height.description: Height of players of this type in number of platform blocks.

acceleration.name: Acceleration
acceleration.description: Rate at which the players of this type increase their movement speed

bounceFactor.name: Wall Bounce
bounceFactor.description: Factor of speed loss when bouncing off of walls.

stepReach.name: Step Reach
stepReach.description: Maximum height of steps that players of this type can climb.

hugMomentum.name: Hug Momentum Threshold
hugMomentum.description: Speed under which players of this type will stick to a wall and slide along it instead of bouncing off it.

appearance.name: Appearance
appearance.description: Model to visually represent this type of player.

spinSpeed.name: Spin Speed
spinSpeed.description: Speed at which players of this type spin as they move.  This is purely a visual effect.

xInput.name: Input X Movement
xInput.description: Input controlling movement of players of this type along the X axis.

yInput.name: Input Y Movement
yInput.description: Input controlling movement of players of this type along the Y axis.

thrustInput.name: Input Thrust
thrustInput.description: Input for doubling movement speed of players of this type.

orientation.name: View Orientation
orientation.description: Movement inputs are adjusted according to angle of the specified view.  This should be the view that is visible to the viewer controlling the player craft.

onRespawn.name: Action on Respawn
onRespawn.description: Action to perform upon respawning after being destroyed.

onFallImpact.name: Action on Surface Impact
onFallImpact.description: Action to perform upon falling onto a floor surface that doesn't cause the player to bounce.

onFallBounce.name: Action on Surface Bounce
onFallBounce.description: Action to perform upon falling onto a floor surface that causes the player to bounce.

onWallBounce.name: Action on Wall Bounce
onWallBounce.description: Action to perform upon bouncing from hitting a wall.

onLeaveSurface.name: Action on Leave Surface
onLeaveSurface.description: Action to perform upon leaving a surface.

onApex.name: Action on Jump Apex
onApex.description: Action to perform upon reaching the apex of a jump.

respawnDelay.name: Respawn Delay
respawnDelay.description: Amount of time in milliseconds after being destroyed before player craft respawns.

Terrain.description: Terrain is used for constructing fixed platforms, walls, water, and other shapes that the player and other objects can traverse.

friction.name: Surface Friction
friction.description: Rate at which objects moving along surfaces of this terrain type lose speed.

grip.name: Surface Grip
grip.description: Factor at which movement changes to objects moving along surfaces of this terrain type are applied.  A value of 1 provides normal control.  A value less than 1 makes surfaces more slippery and a value of 0 nullifies control of objects on the surface.

floorBounce.name: Surface Bounce
floorBounce.description: Factor at which objects hitting surfaces of this terrain type are propelled upwards.  A value of 1 causes objects to reach the same height as they fell from.  A value less than 1 causes objects to lose height on each bounce.  A value of 0 means that objects will not bounce when hitting this surface.

bounceFactor.name: Wall Bounce
bounceFactor.description: Factor at which objects hitting walls of this terrain type will lose speed.

allowRespawn.name: Allow Respawn
allowRespawn.description: Determines whether objects are allowed spawn on surfaces of this terrain type.

solid.name: Solid
solid.description: Determines whether elements of this terrain type are solid or not.  Non-solid terrain types are used for things that allow objects to pass through it such as water.

onTouch.name: Action on Touch
onTouch.description: Action to perform when surfaces of this terrain type are touched by any object.

onFallImpact.name: Action on Impact
onFallImpact.description: Action to perform when surfaces of this terrain type are fell onto by any object.

surface.name: Surface Appearance
surface.description: Pattern to visually represent surfaces of this terrain type.

northWall.name: North Wall Appearance
northWall.description: Pattern to visually represent north-facing walls of this terrain type.

southWall.name: South Wall Appearance
southWall.description: Pattern to visually represent south-facing walls of this terrain type.

westWall.name: West Wall Appearance
westWall.description: Pattern to visually represent west-facing walls of this terrain type.

eastWall.name: East Wall Appearance
eastWall.description: Pattern to visually represent east-facing walls of this terrain type.

TerrainState.description: Terrain states are used for controlling the presence of individual terrain elements via the definition of conditions that include one or more of these states.  Terrain states are also exposed as boolean values in order that they can also be used to control other things, such as lifts.

state.name: Initial State
state.description: Initial value of this terrain state.

hint.name: Hint Action
hint.description: Action to be performed that provides a hint for this terrain state.

iconScale.name: Icon Scale
iconScale.description: Scale of the icon representing this terrain state.

ThemeSet.description: Each theme set contains multiple themes, where each theme contains the same set of elements (textures, colours, etc.), but the appearance of each element varies in each theme.  A theme set may be applied to a zone type, such that each zone of that type applies a theme from the set.  This is useful for setting different colours for each room.

TextureElements.name: Texture Elements
TextureElements.description: Texture elements of each theme in this set.

element.name: Texture Element
element.description: A texture or colour element of each theme in this set.

texture.name: Texture
texture.description: The texture used for this element in this theme.

colour.name: Colour
colour.description: The colour used for this element in this theme.

TextureElementAdd.name: Texture Element
TextureElementAdd.description: Add a texture element for each theme in this set.

ColourElements.name: Colour Elements
ColourElements.description: Colour elements of each theme in this set.

ColourElementAdd.name: Colour Element
ColourElementAdd.description: Add a colour elements for each theme in this set.

Theme.name: Themes
Theme.description: An available themes within this set.

ThemeAdd.name: Themes
ThemeAdd.description: Add a themes to this set.

id.name: Name
id.description: Name of the theme within this set.

World.description: Worlds in which an actual game takes place.  Each world consists of one or more zones (or rooms), each of which can contain elements for the player to traverse.  Note that worlds can also be used for purposes other than gameplay.  For example, worlds can also be used to construct HUD icons, such as the compass, clue, and switch states on the screen.

gravity.name: Gravity
gravity.description: The rate at which falling objects increase their fall speed.

slopeForce.name: Slope Effect
slopeForce.description: The rate at which objects on slopes increase speed in the downward direction of the slope.  This rate is set on the basis of a slope of a single block step in height.

bounceControl.name: Bounce Control
bounceControl.description: A simulated time window in which the movement control is allowed for an object during a bounce from a surface.  This allows a small degree of control while continuously bouncing on surfaces.

Content.name: Content
Content.description: Edit the content of this world.

Editing.name: Editor Settings
Editing.description: Customize the editing functionality of this world.  Note these properties have no impact on the runtime behaviour of this world.

basicProperties.name: Advanced Object Properties
basicProperties.description: Determines whether additional advanced properties of various elements are exposed in the editor.  When disabled, only legacy properties are exposed.  When enabled, extra properties are shown that expose additional functionality, such as lift movement speed, lift pause times, invisible terrain, ghost terrain, etc.

automaticZoneManagement.name: Automatic Zone Type
automaticZoneManagement.description: Type of zone to use for automatic zone management.  If no type of zone is selected, then automatic zone management is disabled for this world and zones must be drawn manually.

automaticZoneXSize.name: Default Zone Width
automaticZoneXSize.description: Width in surface tiles of automatically created zones (west to east).

automaticZoneYSize.name: Default Zone Length
automaticZoneYSize.description: Length in surface tiles of automatically created zones (south to north).

automaticZoneZSize.name: Default Zone Height
automaticZoneZSize.description: Height in block steps of automatically created zones (bottom to top).

defaultThemeSet.name: Default Zone Theme Set
defaultThemeSet.description: Theme set applied to automatically created zones.

editorMinX.name: West Boundary
editorMinX.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend westward.

editorMaxX.name: East Boundary
editorMaxX.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend eastward.

editorMinY.name: South Boundary
editorMinY.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend southward.

editorMaxY.name: North Boundary
editorMaxY.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend northward.

editorMinZ.name: Depth Boundary
editorMinZ.description: Maximum distance in block steps to which this world can extend downward.

editorMaxZ.name: Height Boundary
editorMaxZ.description: Maximum distance in block steps to which this world can extend upward.

defaultWorldEditorTool.name: Default Editing Tool
defaultWorldEditorTool.description: The editor tool selected by default when editing the content of this world.

editorTool.name: Editing Tool
editorTool.description: A tool to be used for editing this world.

EditingEditingToolAdd.name: Editing Tool
EditingEditingToolAdd.description: Add a tool to be used for editing this world.

WorldView.description: Views of worlds that can be presented on the screen.

world.name: World
world.description: The world to be display by this view.

camera.name: Camera
camera.description: The camera through which to view the world.

type.name: ZoneViewType
type.description: Determine how zones are visually represented within this world.

zoom.name: Zoom
zoom.description: A factor by which the world in this view is zoomed.

Zone.description: Different types of rooms that can be used within a world.

ZoneObject.description: Object types with behaviour that can be customized via the application of various "traits".  This is an experimental type of resource that implements an Entity-Component-System design.  It is intended to replace the existing fixed object types, such as lifts, aliens, pick-ups, etc. with a single component type that includes enough flexibility to cover all of these functionalities and much more.  This component type is experimental and unused in the existing game.
